issues.title: Raw palette color used for star icon — should use semantic token
issues.description: The cover star icon uses `text-yellow-500` (raw Tailwind palette) instead of a semantic token. The project has `text-warning` available which maps to `--warning` and conveys the 'selected/favorite' intent semantically.
issues.fix: Replace `text-yellow-500` with `text-warning`. Also update the two E2E tests (apps/auth/e2e/screenshot-proof.spec.ts:197 and apps/auth/e2e/studio-ux-improvements.spec.ts:187) which assert `toHaveClass(/text-yellow-500/)` to use a `data-*` attribute or `aria-pressed` for state detection instead of a class name, per e2e-selectors.md.

issues.title: E2E tests assert state via Tailwind class — violates e2e-selectors rule
issues.description: `await expect(starIcon).toHaveClass(/text-yellow-500/)` is asserting UI state by checking a Tailwind utility class. Per e2e-selectors.md, state assertions must use ARIA attributes or `data-*` attributes, never CSS classes.
issues.fix: Add `data-cover={watchedImages?.[index]?.is_cover}` (or `aria-pressed`) on the Star button in InlineImageCarousel.tsx, then assert `toHaveAttribute('data-cover', 'true')` in the E2E tests instead of `toHaveClass(/text-yellow-500/)`.

issues.title: E2E test asserts state via Tailwind class — duplicate violation
issues.description: Same pattern as STYLE-002: `await expect(starIcon).toHaveClass(/text-yellow-500/)` checks Tailwind class for state. Both test files originate from the same underlying component issue.
issues.fix: Same fix as STYLE-002 — propagate `data-cover` attribute to the SVG or its wrapper and use `toHaveAttribute` in the test.

issues.title: apps/auth, apps/landing, apps/playground use `@source` for app-only scanning
issues.description: Three apps (auth, landing, playground) have `@source "../……/**/*.{ts,tsx}"` which scans only their own source tree. The shared `packages/ui/src/styles/globals.css` (imported via `@import "ui/globals"`) already includes `@source` for the ui and app-components packages, so classes used in those packages should be generated. However, the inconsistency with other apps (store, admin, studio, payments all add the explicit `@source "../../../../packages/ui/src/**/*.{ts,tsx}"` in their own globals.css) makes the intent unclear and could cause confusion. Aligning all apps to the same pattern improves maintainability.
issues.fix: Add `@source "../../../../packages/ui/src/**/*.{ts,tsx}";` to apps/auth/src/app/globals.css, apps/landing/src/app/globals.css, and apps/playground/src/app/globals.css to match the pattern used by store/admin/studio/payments. This makes the scanning intent explicit regardless of how the ui/globals import resolves @source paths.

issues.title: Raw palette colors in playground debug page (intentional/dev-only context)
issues.description: The env debug page uses `text-red-500`, `text-blue-500`, and `text-green-600` raw palette colors. While playground has relaxed quality expectations per the monorepo rules, these could use semantic tokens (`text-destructive`, `text-info`, `text-success`) for consistency.
issues.fix: Replace `text-red-500` with `text-destructive`, `text-blue-500` with `text-info`, `text-green-600` with `text-success`. This is low priority since it is a playground dev-only page.

patterns.pattern: Dynamic CSS variables for themed components
patterns.description: Several components (store/landing/studio) use inline `style={{ backgroundColor: 'var(--pink)', color: 'var(--candy-text)' }}` for per-product/per-category theming. This is the correct pattern when runtime values come from CSS variables rather than hardcoded hex colors. No violations found.
patterns.assessment: Correct usage — CSS variable strings inline are intentional per the project rules.

patterns.description: All 7 apps import `@import 'ui/globals'` which provides a single source of truth for CSS variables, theme, semantic classes, and source scanning. This is well-implemented.
patterns.assessment: Good architectural pattern — CSS is centralized in packages/ui/src/styles/.

patterns.description: The vast majority of app code correctly uses semantic tokens (bg-primary, text-destructive, text-success, text-warning, text-muted-foreground, etc.) instead of raw palette colors. The only violation found was the yellow star icon.
patterns.assessment: Strong compliance with semantic color rules across all apps.
